title: Brain Soul Dichotomy Philosophical Perspectives
wikitext:
[]: Brain/Soul-Dichotomy: Philosophical Perspectives Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:00 pm Sort of a continuation from the discussion regarding the Brain/Soul dichotomy in Evangelion Units. The questions at hand appear to be twofold: Firstly: Reichu wrote: The only philosophy that is relevant is whichever one the Eva universe seems to directly support.

[]: Post by DIDDY » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:04 pm What?

[]: Post by Allemann » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:09 pm Substance dualism, though in this case, souls posses extension as one of their properties. BTW, it's sol i psism.

[]: Re: Brain/Soul-Dichotomy: Philosophical Perspectives Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:32 pm ran1 wrote: And if I am being too unclear here, just say the word and I will try to clean this introduction up a bit.

[]: Re: Brain/Soul-Dichotomy: Philosophical Perspectives Post by Allemann » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:42 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It would probably help to clear up what Existential Sollpisism is for the unread amongst us (myself included), then show how it applies to the workings of a berserk Evangelion, then perhaps contrast that with the alternative theories being proposed.

[]: Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:45 pm It would probably help to clear up what Existential Sollpisism is for the unread amongst us (myself included), then show how it applies to the workings of a berserk Evangelion, then perhaps contrast that with the alternative theories being proposed.

[]: Re: Brain/Soul-Dichotomy: Philosophical Perspectives Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:47 pm Allemann wrote: I've read the term for the first time myself. It's meaningful as "singing chair".

[]: Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:48 pm As I said, eidted definiton in first post

[]: Post by Allemann » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:53 pm Evangelion and solipsism? We see the entire show from a third person perspective. There are other minds. You can't even bind existentialism with solipsism. While existentialism emphasizes individuality and subjectivity, it doesn't deny there are other persons.

[]: Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 30, 2010 5:58 pm You can't even bind existentialism with solipsism. While existentialism emphasizes individuality and subjectivity, it doesn't deny there are other persons

[]: Post by Allemann » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:03 pm ran1 wrote: In Episode 16, Kaji notes that its impossible to "know" someone else entirely. I would say that its a posit that empowers individuality while relegating others to a status less than their own due to one's incomplete knowledge of the Other. In fact, that kind of viewpoint is at the core of some of Shinji's problems with relating to people. But once again, if anyone could propose a better term for what I'm implying, by all means. I don't claim to be an expert in this matter, and I would rather learn than defend an incorrect proposition to the ground.

[]: Post by Legendary » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:03 pm Solipsism says that no one else can be known and that while light versions suggest they MIGHT exist, it is quite firm that there is no proof. Kaji makes no such claim. He doesn't say that women are unreal. He says you can't know them.

[]: Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:05 pm Allemann wrote: Incomplete knowledge of other individuals doesn't bring skepticism about other minds besides one's own, nor does it follow that objective facts are hidden from humans.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:08 pm This is all, of course, completely and totally irrelevant to brain/soul dichotomy. It just means that people in the Evaverse aren't psychic.

[]: Post by Allemann » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:10 pm ran1 wrote: Though it does leave room for questioning this fact, and when looking at the unstable and damaged minds of Evangelion's characters, it very well remains in question until the onset of Instrumentality.

[]: Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:10 pm This is all, of course, completely and totally irrelevant to brain/soul dichotomy. It just means that people in the Evaverse aren't psychic.

[]: Post by NemZ » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:20 pm Allemann wrote: Substance dualism, though in this case, souls posses extension as one of their properties.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:24 pm Although it's probably more likely that the EoTV ending was, accurately, referring to simply changing your perspective on things. To avoid having that argument again, let's just keep in mind that at best, the whole "change reality" thing only came up during a big psychic dream thing, and may not be palpable to the ordinary, standard reality outside of Third Impact.

[]: Post by NemZ » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:35 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Although it's probably more likely that the EoTV ending was, accurately, referring to simply changing your perspective on things.

[]: Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 30, 2010 6:38 pm ran1 wrote: Well, the first question is what philosophy is most directly influencing the Eva universe. From there, one can move onto the brain/soul dichotomy.
